title: 10 best herbal teas for toothache
intro_desc: Herbal teas for toothache are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that can help alleviate toothache symptoms. They work by reducing inflammation, numbing the pain, and promoting overall oral health. Some examples of herbal teas that help with toothache include chamomile, clove, and peppermint teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that can numb the pain. Peppermint tea has antiseptic and analgesic properties that can soothe inflamed gums and provide relief from toothache. By using herbal teas for toothache, individuals can find natural and effective relief from toothache symptoms, improving their quality of life and overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow teas helps with toothache because it contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a tea, salicin can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth, providing relief from toothaches. The soothing effects of white willow tea can also calm irritated gums and mucous membranes, making it an effective remedy for relieving toothache discomfort.
remedy_properties: Salicylic acid: This compound, a natural analog of aspirin, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with toothaches.

Tannins: The tannins in white willow tea have antiseptic properties that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to the toothache, reducing inflammation and preventing infection.
Methyl salicylate: This compound, also known as wintergreen oil, has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help numb the pain of a toothache and reduce swelling.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the white willow plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salix alba leaves from sustainable sources weighing about 2 ounces per serving.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed leaves with 8 oz of boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and retain the liquid.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 10-15 minutes before serving, allowing the flavors to mature and blend.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Quaking aspen teas helps with toothache because of its natural antibacterial properties, which help to combat the infection causing the pain. The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce swelling and ease pressure on the affected area, providing temporary relief from discomfort. Additionally, quaking aspen's soothing properties can calm the nerves, helping to numb the pain and promote a sense of calm, making it a natural remedy for toothaches.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: Quaking aspen tea's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce pain, swelling, and inflammation associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in quaking aspen tea, such as salicylic acid and other phenolic acids, can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache, reducing the risk of complications and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Quaking aspen tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the gums and surrounding tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oral health issues like periodontitis and further exacerbate toothache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and swelling associated with toothaches.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, have antibacterial properties that can help combat infection-causing bacteria in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried leaves and stems of quaking aspen tree.
Measure one teaspoon of dried quaking aspen herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the medicinal properties and flavors.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth and serve hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When brewed into a tea, yarrow's active compounds are released and absorbed by the body, helping to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth and gums. This can provide relief from the sharp, shooting sensations associated with toothaches caused by inflammation, abscesses, or tooth decay. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections that contribute to toothache pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.
Antibacterial: Yarrow tea contains antibacterial compounds that can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection of the affected area.
Pain-relieving: Yarrow's analgesic properties can help alleviate toothache pain by numbing the nerve endings and reducing sensitivity, providing temporary relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids and lignans, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the mouth.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are rich in essential oils that provide analgesic and antibacterial effects, making them effective in relieving toothache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow leaves stems and flowers for one ounce of tea.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a heat-resistant container at ratio one part yarrow to eight parts water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or individual cups.
Enjoy the warm tea hot or allow it to cool for a refreshing and soothing beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the mouth. When brewed into a tea, these oils can be applied topically to the affected area or consumed orally to help alleviate toothache symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from dental discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing tooth infections and abscesses, helping to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with toothaches by reducing the body's natural response to injury or infection.
Analgesic properties: Thymol has been found to have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb the affected area and provide temporary relief from pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help alleviate gum inflammation and kill bacteria causing toothaches.
Leaves: Leaves contain a high concentration of essential oils, including thymol, which has natural analgesic properties that soothe tooth pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves by pinching off stems from mature plants in the morning.
Rinse the harvested thyme leaves with cool water to remove any dirt or debris gently.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh thyme leaves with 8 ounces boiling water steep for.
Allow the tea mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve slowly.
Discard the solids and serve the herbal thyme tea hot or let it cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary teas helps with toothache because it has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe a sore tooth. When you drink rosemary tea, its active compounds such as rosmarinic acid and camphor enter the bloodstream and travel to the affected area, reducing swelling and pain. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache, promoting healing and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache.

Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial agents in rosemary tea, including rosmarinic acid, combat bacteria that can cause tooth infection and abscesses, reducing the severity of toothache symptoms.
Pain-relieving properties: Rosemary tea's essential oils, particularly camphor, have a mild anesthetic effect, helping to numb the pain associated with toothaches, providing temporary relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Bark: The bark of the rosemary plant contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort from toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality fresh or dried rosemarinus officinalis leaves and flowers from reputable sources.
Measure two teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves or one tablespoon of fresh rosemary leaves per cup of boiling water.
Bring filtered water to a boil and then reduce heat to simmer for five minutes to steep the rosemary leaves.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or mug, discarding the solids.
Allow the herbal tea to cool slightly before serving hot or chilled, with honey and lemon to taste if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids help to reduce swelling and ease pain, providing quick relief from a throbbing toothache. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can help to relax tense muscles in the jaw and face, further contributing to a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help combat bacteria that may be causing the toothache, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce swelling and pain associated with a toothache by suppressing prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Anesthetic properties: The esters present in lavender oil, particularly linalool, have been shown to have mild anesthetic effects, which may help numb the affected area and provide temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them effective against toothaches.
Seeds: Containing antibacterial properties, seeds can be used to create a tea that helps combat infections causing toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia, use 1 tablespoon for every 8 ounces water.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle and let it cool down to 205°f to 212°f.
Steep the lavender flowers in the hot water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture using a tea infuser or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Serve the herbal english lavender tea warm or chilled, with honey and lemon if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with toothache because its natural antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the mouth. The tea's soothing effects can also calm the nerves, providing relief from sensitivity and discomfort caused by tooth decay or gum disease. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties may help to relax muscles in the jaw and face, further reducing tension and alleviating toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Lemon balm's antiseptic properties help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to toothaches, by killing or inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause infection.

Anxiolytic: The anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects of lemon balm tea can help alleviate stress and anxiety related to dental pain, allowing patients to relax and potentially reducing their perception of discomfort.

Pain-relieving: Lemon balm contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic (pain-reducing) properties, helping to numb the affected area and provide relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Bark: The bark of lemon balm contains tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and numb the affected area, providing relief from toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated.
Measure one tablespoon of dried herbs or two tablespoons of fresh herbs for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured herbs with boiling water and steep for five to seven minutes to release flavors and aromas.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot to remove solids.
Enjoy the herbal lemon balm tea hot, sweetened with honey if desired, within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in oregano, such as carvacrol and thymol, work to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with toothaches. Additionally, the antibacterial compounds in oregano help combat bacteria that can cause infections, which often contribute to tooth pain. Drinking oregano tea may also help promote healthy gums and teeth by reducing inflammation and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Oregano tea's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth, which may help alleviate toothache pain caused by infections or abscesses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in oregano tea, such as carvacrol and thymol, can help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Oregano tea's high antioxidant content may help protect the teeth and gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oral health issues like tooth decay and gum disease that can lead to toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the oregano plant have antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.
Bark: The bark of the oregano plant contains compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in treating toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried origanum vulgare leaves per cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the measured herbs in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic teapot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds and flavors.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the oregano tea hot, garnished with lemon slices or honey to taste.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Winter savory teas helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce pain and swelling in the mouth, making it an effective remedy for soothing toothaches. Additionally, winter savory's antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be causing the toothache, providing a natural and holistic approach to relief.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Winter savory tea's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections, which can contribute to toothaches, by reducing the growth and spread of bacteria in the mouth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in winter savory tea help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, making it a natural remedy for relieving discomfort and promoting healing.

Antioxidant properties: Winter savory tea's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to oral health issues like tooth decay and gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Winter savory roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain caused by toothaches.
Seeds: The seeds of winter savory contain antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that can help combat infections and promote healing in toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh satureja montana leaves from your garden or purchase dried ones in the market.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried winter savory herb with 1 quart of boiling water for steeping.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well to dissolve the sweetness evenly throughout.
Serve hot tea immediately, or store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort teas helps with toothache because its active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and surrounding areas, providing relief from toothaches caused by inflammation or irritation. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of St John's Wort may help combat infection, further alleviating discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, which can help reduce the risk of infection and promote healing in toothache-related wounds.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in St. John's Wort tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, as well as alleviate symptoms such as gum inflammation.

Analgesic properties: St. John's Wort has been traditionally used to relieve mild to moderate pain, including headaches and toothaches, due to its ability to interact with neurotransmitters that regulate pain perception.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving toothache pain.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and hyperforin, which help reduce inflammation and ease toothache discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried st john's wort flowers into a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Heat fresh water to boiling point in a kettle or on the stovetop and pour over the herb.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea into another cup using the tea infuser or by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Enjoy your st john's wort tea immediately, or let it cool before refrigerating for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat toothache with herbal teas is by using a strong tea made from cloves or chamomile. Soak a cotton ball in the cooled tea and apply it directly to the affected tooth. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. Drinking the tea can also provide internal relief. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can help manage symptoms, they are not a substitute for professional dental care. If the pain persists, you should consult a dentist.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal teas include sore throats and mouth ulcers. For a sore throat, teas made from slippery elm, licorice root, or chamomile can provide relief due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Meanwhile, mouth ulcers can be treated with teas containing calendula or echinacea, which have antibacterial and healing effects. It is important to note that while these herbal teas can help alleviate symptoms, they should not replace proper medical attention when necessary. Always consult with a healthcare professional for appropriate treatment.
